item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was picking up some parallels with the human immune system but thought I was reading too much into it…so glad to see I wasn't! Great idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, wow, it's a human immune system? Holy crap, that makes all the sense. :D

Even without knowing that interpretation, this was a great pile of what-the-fuck and I loved it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
EXTREME HOUSE HUNTING!

In all seriousness, this was a delightful read. How the houses operated together light the various components of a cell was quite intriguing, and the infection contracted by the children at the end of the article was a nice twist.

+1. A good way to kick off Series V, weryl.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not actually Jailors per se. Its supposed to be emulating, albeit on a very simple scale, the human immune system.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a little torn on this one. On the one side, the idea and the visuals of this are really good - jailor houses keeping prisoner houses locked up while they are constantly doing mad shit to try and escape. On the other hand, a lot of this feels kind of rough (I feel like it could have used another editing pass), and the note at the end with the virus feels a little loosely connected. I can kind of see what you're going for there, but the mechanics of it when compared to the rest of it don't really add up.

I've ultimately decided to upvote because the image of those big read houses zipping around trying to eat things with their garage doors is great, but I've listed some issues I found below.

Issues
– hide block

A barrier has been constructed 100m around SCP-4243's perimeter.

I feel like something in this sentence is out of order, or it's missing words or something. It reads rather oddly. As I was writing this I worked out its meaning, but because I didn't already know about the brown house perimeter wall I just found this sentence very odd to read.

After an average 2 years after formation,

Double use of "after" here reads poorly.

SCP-4243-RED instances encompass a variety of shapes and styles, ranging from 1850s to 1950s American construction styles.

Double use of "styles" here and general sentence structure makes this feel a little fragmented. Suggest something like "encompass a variety of shapes and styles consistent with typical American housing architecture from the 1850s to 1950s."

by cars autonomously driving to the breach and fusing to the wall.

No mention of cars at all prior to this point, so this threw me a little. Where did they come from? Are they just parked on the roads?

however, no historical records note the existence of either this town or Wallover.

this->the would read better.

ETA: It's been pointed out that I have completely misinterpreted what's going on here, and the ending makes more sense to me now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Been a while since I played with biology. Had the initial prompt "and then the town coagulates" in a tab in my old sandbox for ages. Pumped it out to a full draft in a couple days, and here we go.

Thanks to LilyFlower, DrBleep, OthellotheCat, Cyantreuse, and LordStonefish for looking it over.

Image source is https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Blue_House.JPG
CC 3.0, cropped by me.
